LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT GRANTED COUNSEL GEORGE M. RUSH'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF PAULINE FRY AND ORDERED AS FOLLOWS: 1) COUNSEL RUSH IS TO DELIVER ALL FILES AND MATERIAL DIRECTLY TO PLAINTIFF AT 5PM ON 10/7/03 AND FILE DECLARATION RE: DELIVERY WITH THE COURT. 2) PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO MEET WITH THE PUBLIC GUARDIAN AND TRY TO HIRE ATTORNEY TO REPRESENT HER. 3) COUNSEL RUSH WILL NOTIFY PLAINTIFF OF ALL FUTURE HEARINGS. ORDER SIGNED AFTER THE HEARING. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR# 1558.

Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-07-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
George M. Rush, Esq., 393-8005, moving to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Pauline Fry
Pauline Fry appearing in propria persona, unopposed to the motion

TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE:
John Frederick Van De Poel of VAN DE POEL & LEVY 925/934-6102, for defendant and will be availible by phone

RULING:
* The Court met counsel Rush in chambers, off the record.
** In chambers, the Court, counsel Rush, Plaintiff Fry and court staff were present, Counsel Van De Poel waived appearance. The Court granted counsel George M. Rush's motion to be relieved as counsel for PLAINTIFF PAULINE FRY and ordered as follows: 1) Counsel Rush is to deliver all files and material directly to plaintiff at 5pm on 10/7/03 and file declaration re: delivery with the Court. 2) Plaintiff is directed to meet with the public guardian and try to hire attorney to represent her. 3) Counsel Rush will notify plaintiff of all future hearings. ORDER SIGNED after the hearing. (301/JLW/JU) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
